message:
test(e2e): add safety blocked command E2E test (bd-ublgd)

Add TestSafetyBlocked_JSON verifying the `ntm safety blocked --json`
command correctly reads and returns blocked command entries from the
JSONL log.

New components:
- SafetyBlockedResponse struct mirroring JSON output
- runSafetyBlocked() helper with --hours and --limit flag support
- writeBlockedLog() helper to seed test blocked.jsonl entries
- Test creates a blocked entry for a destructive command pattern,
  writes log, invokes CLI, asserts count >= 1 and correct
  command/pattern fields

Also updates beads: closes bd-ublgd, moves bd-z7ses to in_progress.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"bd-u4cfd","title":"Feature: Redaction middleware for REST/WS (requests, responses, logs)","description":"## Summary
As NTM’s REST/WS/web platform expands, ensure sensitive strings are not accidentally echoed back via API responses, websocket events, or server logs.

## Scope
- Apply redaction engine to:
  - request bodies where messages/prompts are accepted
  - response bodies/events where prompts/outputs may be returned
  - server-side logs that may include user text

## Design Notes
- Redaction should be opt-in configurable but recommended by default for remote binds.
- Preserve debuggability: include redaction summary counts/categories.

## Dependencies
- Reuse the unified redaction engine (bd-3cgwv feature).

## Acceptance Criteria
- No raw synthetic secret strings appear in REST/WS payloads when redaction enabled.
- Unit tests for middleware.
- Integration tests for at least one REST endpoint and one WS event.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:34:06.638063553Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf","updated_at":"2026-02-02T04:09:33.574624711Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T04:09:33.574559810Z","close_reason":"Feature is complete: REST middleware (bd-vb1e5), WS redaction (bd-1s8x2), and integration tests (bd-15zf0) are closed and on main.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-u4cfd","depends_on_id":"bd-2ku3r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:39Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-u91ot","title":"Feature: Findings Deduplication UI","description":"## Summary
UI for reviewing and managing duplicate/similar findings across modes before synthesis.

## Rationale
Multiple modes often surface the same issue. Deduplication improves synthesis quality while preserving provenance.

## Implementation
- Use deterministic similarity + clustering from the dedupe engine.
- Dashboard shows clusters with:
  - Primary finding + related findings
  - Source modes + provenance
  - Merge/split controls
- CLI: `ntm ensemble dedupe --interactive` and `--auto --threshold`.

## Logging
- Log cluster sizes, merge actions, and threshold used.

## Tests
- Unit: clustering + merge/split operations
- Integration: UI renders clusters
- E2E: CLI interactive + auto dedupe flows

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Similar findings detected and grouped deterministically
- [ ] Dashboard cluster UI works
- [ ] Merge/split operations preserve provenance
- [ ] CLI interactive mode functional
- [ ] Threshold is configurable and logged

## Dependencies
- OutputSchema (bd-1wnxl)
- Provenance tracking (bd-364w3)
- Redundancy normalization (bd-254do)
- Dashboard panel (bd-121pa)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:07:41.702367333Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T22:00:41.912515679Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-u91ot","depends_on_id":"bd-121pa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-u91ot","depends_on_id":"bd-1h4ox","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-u91ot","depends_on_id":"bd-1wnxl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-u91ot","depends_on_id":"bd-254do","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-u91ot","depends_on_id":"bd-364w3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ubhl","title":"E2E Tests: ntm init workflow with detailed logging","description":"E2E Tests for ntm init workflow with detailed logging.

## Test Scenarios

1. Fresh project initialization
   - Create temp directory
   - Run ntm init
   - Verify all directories created
   - Verify config files valid
   - Log each step with timestamps

2. Re-initialization with --force
   - Init a project
   - Modify config
   - Re-init with --force
   - Verify clean state restored

3. Init with existing partial structure
   - Create partial .ntm directory
   - Run ntm init
   - Verify missing pieces filled in
   - Existing files preserved

4. Integration with flywheel tools
   - Run ntm init
   - Verify Agent Mail can connect
   - Verify CM can initialize
   - Verify bv can read project

## Detailed Logging Requirements
```
[E2E] Step N/TOTAL: DESCRIPTION
[E2E] Creating directory: PATH
[E2E] Writing config: PATH (N bytes)
[E2E] PASS: TEST_NAME in DURATION
[E2E] FAIL: TEST_NAME - expected X, got Y
```

## Test Artifact Collection
- Capture all stdout/stderr
- Save config files on failure
- Create test report JSON with pass/fail/timing

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All 4 test scenarios implemented
2. Fresh init creates all expected directories
3. --force restores clean state
4. Partial init fills missing pieces
5. Flywheel integration verified
6. Detailed step-by-step logging
7. Test report JSON generated on completion
8. Exit code 0 on all pass, non-zero on any failure","acceptance_criteria":"1. E2E tests verify ntm init workflow
2. Default config created correctly
3. Session directory structure created
4. Config validation works
5. Detailed logging throughout
6. Test duration under 3 minutes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:12:18.220970047Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-31T01:34:20.839598436Z","closed_at":"2026-01-31T01:34:20.839531462Z","close_reason":"Implemented init E2E scenarios w/ step logging + JSON report/artifacts","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ubhl","depends_on_id":"bd-1z8s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:39Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-ubhl","depends_on_id":"bd-38gl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:39Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-ughtn","title":"Feature: Mode Explanation Cards","description":"## Summary
Provide explanation cards for each reasoning mode: what it does, when to use it, and example outputs.

## Rationale
80 modes is overwhelming. Cards reduce trial-and-error and help users build better ensembles.

## Implementation
- ModeCard data lives alongside the mode catalog (validated, deterministic).
- `ntm modes explain <mode>` renders a card (human + JSON).
- Dashboard/TUI shows cards on hover/click.
- Dry-run preview links to card summaries.

## Logging
- Log explain command usage + mode_id

## Tests
- Unit: every mode has a complete card
- Unit: JSON schema validation for cards
- E2E: CLI explain output formatting

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Every mode has a complete explanation card
- [ ] CLI explain command works (human + JSON)
- [ ] Dashboard shows interactive cards
- [ ] Cards include usage guidance + examples

## Dependencies
- Requires embedded mode catalog (bd-11mtt)
- Requires mode metadata extensions (bd-3806s)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:07:25.577850463Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T02:48:56.593539002Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T02:48:56.593381265Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ughtn","depends_on_id":"bd-11mtt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-ughtn","depends_on_id":"bd-1h4ox","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-ughtn","depends_on_id":"bd-3806s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-uptv","title":"E2E Tests: Round-robin distribution with multiple agent types","description":"## Test Coverage
E2E test of round-robin strategy via `ntm assign --strategy=round-robin` across mixed agent types.

## Test Scenario
1. Spawn mixed agents: --cc=2 --cod=2 --gmi=1
2. Create 10 test beads
3. Run: `ntm assign <session> --strategy=round-robin --limit=10`
4. Verify even distribution

## Expected Distribution (10 beads / 5 agents)
- Claude 1: beads 1, 6
- Claude 2: beads 2, 7
- Codex 1: beads 3, 8
- Codex 2: beads 4, 9
- Gemini 1: beads 5, 10

## Verification
- Each agent gets exactly 2 beads
- Beads assigned in order
- No duplicates

## Logging Requirements
```bash
log_e2e() {
    echo "[E2E-ROUNDROBIN] $(date -Iseconds) test=$1 step=$2 status=$3 details=\"$4\""
}

# Example usage:
log_e2e "round_robin_distribute" "spawn_agents" "start" "cc=2 cod=2 gmi=1"
log_e2e "round_robin_distribute" "spawn_agents" "complete" "panes=5"
log_e2e "round_robin_distribute" "create_beads" "complete" "count=10"
log_e2e "round_robin_distribute" "assign" "start" "strategy=round-robin limit=10"
log_e2e "round_robin_distribute" "assign" "complete" "assigned=10 agents=5"
log_e2e "round_robin_distribute" "verify" "checking" "agent=cc_1 expected=2"
log_e2e "round_robin_distribute" "verify" "pass" "all agents got exactly 2 beads"
```

## Test File
tests/e2e/test_round_robin_assign.sh

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All agents receive equal number of beads
- [ ] Assignment order is deterministic
- [ ] Robot output JSON is valid
- [ ] Detailed logging at each step
- [ ] Test passes in CI environment","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:22:00.649949242Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T02:46:35.128774975Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T02:46:35.128728166Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-uptv","depends_on_id":"bd-l4y4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-uptv","depends_on_id":"bd-pjjk7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-uzasi","title":"Task: Implement mode output caching system","description":"## Implementation
Add caching layer for mode outputs (prefer existing internal/ensemble files; add new file only if needed):

1. CacheManager:
   - Disk-backed store under `.ntm/ensemble-cache/`
   - `CacheKey = ContextHash + ModeID + ModeVersion + ConfigHash`
   - Methods: Get/Put/Invalidate/Stats

2. Invalidation:
   - Context hash change
   - Mode version bump
   - TTL expiry (default 24h, configurable)
   - `--no-cache` explicit bypass

3. CLI integration:
   - `--use-cache/--no-cache`
   - `ntm ensemble cache stats` and `cache clear`

## Logging
- Log hit/miss + invalidation reason per mode

## Tests
- Unit: key derivation + TTL expiry
- Integration: cache persists across runs
- E2E: rerun uses cache; `--no-cache` forces refresh

## Dependencies
- Requires ModeOutput schema (bd-1wnxl)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:40:30.293022655Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T21:55:07.345908312Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-uzasi","depends_on_id":"bd-1wnxl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-uzasi","depends_on_id":"bd-3uydb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-z6py","title":"Task: Create CM client module","description":"## Purpose
Create a Go module for interacting with CASS Memory (CM) CLI.

## Module Location
internal/cm/client.go

## CMClient Interface
type CMClient struct {
    executor Executor
    timeout  time.Duration
}

func (c *CMClient) IsAvailable() bool
func (c *CMClient) Context(projectDir, taskDescription string) (*CMContextResponse, error)

## CMContextResponse Struct
type CMContextResponse struct {
    RelevantBullets  []MemoryRule    
    AntiPatterns     []MemoryRule    
    HistorySnippets  []HistoryEntry  
    SuggestedQueries []string        
}

type MemoryRule struct {
    ID       string
    Content  string
    Category string
}

## Acceptance Criteria
- Client wraps cm CLI invocation
- Parses JSON response correctly
- Handles cm not installed gracefully
- Respects timeout configuration

## Technical Notes
Use exec.Command with --json flag
Follow patterns from internal/cass/ client","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-z8gbe","title":"xf Integration: X/Twitter Archive Search for Knowledge Retrieval","description":"Integrate xf for X/Twitter archive search in robot mode workflows.

## Features

- Search X/Twitter archive
- Multiple search modes (keyword, semantic, hybrid)
- Result relevance scoring
- Robot mode JSON output

## Robot Mode API

```
ntm --robot-xf-search=QUERY [--xf-mode=MODE] [--limit=N]
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. xf binary detection works
2. Search executes correctly
3. Results formatted as JSON
4. Search modes work (keyword/semantic/hybrid)
5. Limit option caps results
6. Dashboard widget shows search results
7. Config section for all options
8. Unit tests for all components (80% coverage)
9. E2E tests verify search integration","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:41:24.484753120Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:06:55.638436036Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-z8gbe","depends_on_id":"bd-2jsvq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-zcft","title":"Task: Create Ollama adapter for local LLM communication","description":"## Overview
Implement an adapter that connects NTM to local Ollama instances for running local LLMs.

## Deliverables
1. Create internal/agent/ollama/adapter.go:
   - OllamaAdapter struct implementing AgentBackend interface
   - Connect(host string) error
   - SendPrompt(prompt string) (Response, error)
   - StreamResponse(prompt string) (<-chan Token, error)
   - ListModels() ([]Model, error)
   - PullModel(name string) error

2. Ollama API integration:
   - POST /api/generate for completions
   - POST /api/chat for chat format
   - GET /api/tags for model listing
   - POST /api/pull for model download

3. Response handling:
   - Parse streaming JSON responses
   - Extract token content, completion status
   - Track token generation rate

4. Error handling:
   - Connection errors (Ollama not running)
   - Model not found (suggest pull)
   - Context length exceeded
   - GPU memory exhausted

## Implementation Notes
- Use net/http for API calls
- Handle NDJSON streaming
- Support custom Ollama host via NTM_OLLAMA_HOST

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can connect to local Ollama instance
- Prompts sent and responses received
- Streaming works for real-time output
- Errors handled gracefully with helpful messages
- Unit tests with mock Ollama server","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:29:39.666892901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T23:06:26.618222933Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T23:06:26.616414988Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zcft","depends_on_id":"bd-1t8p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"}]}
